title: cerinthe minor subsp. cleiostoma
intro: Cerinthe minor subsp. cleiostoma is a subspecies of the lesser blue thistle plant, scientifically known as Cerinthe major subsp. cleiostoma, a flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cerinthe minor subsp. cleiostoma include its ability to relieve bronchitis, coughs, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. The plant's medicinal properties are often utilized in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
